Therefore, all points through which an isotherm passes have the same or equal temperatures at the time indicated. An isotherm at 0 °C is called the freezing level. To find variation or outliers in data, divide the mean absolute deviation by the mean. This will give you an outlier score. An outlier score of less than 0.1 is considered very low. A score of 0.1 to 0.175 is considered pretty low. A score of 0.175 to 0.3 is considered relatively low. A score of 0.3 to 0.45 is considered moderate. A score of 0.45 to 0.6 is considered relatively high. A score of 0.6 to 1 is considered pretty high. A score of 1 and above is considered very high.
